figures.caption: Fig. 1: The emerging commercial skill ecosystem. Skills are increasingly packaged, priced, and distributed as reusable digital assets, creating direct incentives for unauthorized extraction. The example interface in this figure is drawn from Claw Mart, a paid marketplace for AI skills and personas [1].

TABLE III: Experimental results with LAN defense (LLM and NVRecall). Abbreviations follow the definitions in the experimental setup: SC is the scenario strategy, ST is the request-structure strategy, OR is the original response, and LAN is the LLM-and-NVRecall-filtered response.

TABLE VII: Experimental results with sandwich defense. Strategy and metric abbreviations follow the definitions in the experimental setup. Numeric cells use the same low-saturation diverging heatmap style as the main-text heatmap tables.

TABLE VIII: Experimental results with SkillGuard-5 prompt defense. Strategy and metric abbreviations follow the definitions in the experimental setup. Numeric cells use the same low-saturation diverging heatmap style as the main-text heatmap tables.

TABLE VI: Experimental results with instruction defense. Strategy and metric abbreviations follow the definitions in the experimental setup. Numeric cells use the same low-saturation diverging heatmap style as the main-text heatmap tables.

TABLE V: Experimental results with no defense. Strategy and metric abbreviations follow the definitions in the experimental setup. Numeric cells use the same low-saturation diverging heatmap style as the main-text heatmap tables.
Fig. 8: Heatmap of inference-phase defense performance across target models and defense settings, measured by leakage reduction under multiple evaluation metrics.
TABLE I: Commercial-agent leakage comparison between Claude Code and Codex under different scenarios and requeststructure strategies.

TABLE IV: Comparison of translation- and rewrite-based attacks under different output-filtering defenses. Abbreviations follow the experimental setup; OR, LAN, COS, and EM denote original output, LAN-filtered output, cosine-filtered output, and exactmatch-filtered output, respectively.
